title: Name resolution service — pluggable resolver replacing EPMD
purpose: BEAM nodes find each other via EPMD (Erlang Port Mapper Daemon), a standalone process that maps node names to TCP ports on a host. beamr replaces EPMD with a pluggable resolver trait — the default implementation uses DNS SRV records or a static config file, but operators can plug in service discovery (Consul, Kubernetes DNS, etc.).
task: Define the NodeResolver trait and a static resolver implementation. The resolver maps node names to (host, port) pairs.

requirements.title: NodeResolver trait
requirements.spec: Define an async trait for resolving node names to network addresses.
acceptance: NodeResolver trait with async fn resolve(name: &str) -> Result<SocketAddr, ResolveError>
Resolver is Arc<dyn NodeResolver + Send + Sync> for shared use
Test: mock resolver returns configured address

requirements.title: Static resolver implementation
requirements.spec: A resolver backed by a static map of node names to addresses, loaded from configuration.
acceptance: StaticResolver::new(map: HashMap<String, SocketAddr>) creates a resolver
Resolves known names to configured addresses
Test: configure 3 nodes, resolve each, verify addresses

requirements.title: Resolver registration in distribution config
requirements.spec: The distribution subsystem holds a configured resolver, defaulting to StaticResolver with no entries (non-distributed mode).
acceptance: DistributionConfig struct with resolver: Arc<dyn NodeResolver>
Test: default config resolves nothing (non-distributed mode)

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement EPMD compatibility — beamr uses a pluggable resolver, not EPMD
SHALL NOT implement DNS SRV resolution in this brief — that's a future resolver implementation
SHALL NOT open network connections — that's B-114
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
